topSignals.title: Top active signals

portfolio.title: Portfolio
portfolio.tagline: Paper venue · fills and exits on closed candles · never financial advice.

notice.closed: {{symbol}} closed at the latest market price.

positions.empty: No open positions. Fills land here; stops and targets exit them automatically.
positions.scaleOut: Scale out: close half the position at the latest closed price

orders.empty: No working orders. Limit and stop entries wait here until price reaches them, expire by their time-in-force, or are cancelled.

history.empty: No closed trades yet. Every exit — stop, target, trailing, or manual — is recorded here with its realized P&L and R multiple.

watchlist.title: Curated symbol monitor
watchlist.description: Grouped crypto and stock rows with sparkline, last price, active signal chips, alert access, and removal actions.
watchlist.notListed: Not listed yet — add it to the corridor as:

constitution.notAdopted: Not adopted yet — write your rules, then hold yourself to them.
constitution.requireReason: Every trade has a written reason

journal.title: Journal
journal.tagline: The plan you wrote, the trade you took, and what you make of it.
journal.loadError: Could not load the journal. Is the backend running?
journal.empty: No executed paper trades yet. Place a trade with a full plan — stop, target, risk, and reason — and it will appear here for review.
journal.notePlaceholder: What actually happened? What would you repeat — or never do again?
journal.footer: Tags feed the Review coach — honest tagging is what makes the recommendations yours.

missions.title: Missions
missions.tagline: Earned by conduct, not clicks — every criterion reads your real activity.
missions.loadError: Could not load missions. Is the backend running?
missions.noActive: No active missions. Open a category below and start one — active missions pin your next objective and link straight to where you act.

card.slotsFull: All active slots are taken — stand down from one first.

replay.title: Replay missions
replay.description: Stylized recreations of real market episodes. Play them bar by bar and make the calls — the debrief grades the decision, not the outcome.

missions.footer: Completing missions unlocks Academy levels. Community packs can add more via the extension SDK. Educational use only — not financial advice.

backtest.title: Statistically honest validation
backtest.description: Strategy selector, cost model inputs, in-sample vs out-of-sample metrics, and low-sample warnings. Parameter changes rerun the backend quant engine against stored corridor candles.
backtest.runFailed: The backend rerun failed - if data was just ingested, retry in a few seconds.
backtest.defaultRunMessage: Parameter changes rerun against the backend and update the metrics below.

status.running: Running backend backtest with the current cost model and validation split.
status.synced: Metrics below are synced from the backend quant engine using stored corridor candles.
status.noCandles: No stored {{timeframe}} candles for {{symbol}} — this symbol has {{available}} data. Pick one of those timeframes and rerun.

howTo.title: How this works
howTo.intro: It replays one setup's exact entry/stop/target rules over the candles stored on this machine and reports how that setup would have traded — win rate, expectancy in R, equity and drawdown — with fees and slippage charged on every round trip. It validates honestly: the sample is split chronologically into train/test so the headline numbers must survive data the rules never saw, and every run gets the workbench below — cost-stress scenarios, Monte Carlo drawdowns, bias gates, and the AI research review.
howTo.step1: Pick a preset — presets are generated from the engine's live signals (one per symbol/setup the engine currently detects) plus anything you saved earlier.
howTo.step2: Adjust fees, slippage, and the train/test split; the run re-executes instantly.
howTo.step3: Read the out-of-sample row and the workbench before trusting anything else.
howTo.step4Suffix: stores it under Settings → Strategies for re-running later.
howTo.footer: A backtest is evidence about the past, never a promise about the future. The matching Academy track (Backtesting & Statistical Honesty) teaches every number on this screen.

selector.title: Strategy / setup selector
selector.presetsHint: Detected-signal presets appear automatically once the engine has generated signals from stored market data. Saved strategies and the demo are always available above.
selector.composeHint: A strategy here = symbol × setup family × timeframe × costs × validation. Each family's entry/stop/target rules are the engine's published, backtested code (taught in Learn); new rule families come from extensions — never from a free-text box that can't be tested.

prefill.liveBacking: Live market corridor backing is available for {{symbol}} {{timeframe}} via {{provider}}, last updated {{time}} UTC.

empty.title: Start your first statistically honest backtest
empty.description: Choose a strategy source, confirm costs and the train/test split, then run it to see equity, drawdown, out-of-sample metrics, Monte Carlo, and the AI review.
empty.demoNote: The demo runs the built-in trend-pullback rules on stored market data — an educational sample, not a live signal.

error.title: Backtest results unavailable
error.description: The backtest results could not be loaded.

inOut.title: In-sample vs out-of-sample
inOut.overfittingWarning: Overfitting risk warning: out-of-sample win rate trails in-sample by {{gap}} percentage points.
inOut.noOverfitting: In-sample and out-of-sample performance remain close enough to avoid an overfitting warning.

sample.lowWarningBody: This setup has fewer than {{threshold}} trades, so the validation statistics should be treated as unstable.
sample.aboveThreshold: Sample size is above the configured minimum backtest threshold of {{threshold}} trades.

workbench.costStressNote: Same simulation, worse costs. An edge that dies at 2x slippage was never an edge.

language.title: Language
language.label: Display language
language.help: Choose the language for the app interface. AI insights answer in this language too.

common.notFinancialAdvice: Educational use only — not financial advice.
